evals.prompt: what am I missing before I design this? requirements/requirement-gap-analysis/fixtures/two_sentence_feature_request.txt
evals.expected_output: GAP_ANALYSIS.md written next to the input and shown inline: findings grouped by category (functional, non-functional, edge cases, stakeholders, data, compliance), each as a specific question to ask with no confidence score and no overall verdict. Ends by offering to walk through the Blocking questions one at a time.
assertions: GAP_ANALYSIS.md is written next to the input and also shown inline
Findings are grouped by category and each is phrased as a specific question, not a vague 'X is missing' statement
No confidence score is attached to any finding
No overall approve/reject verdict is given
The assistant offers to walk through Blocking questions one at a time after the report

evals.prompt: what should I ask before we build this? requirements/requirement-gap-analysis/fixtures/stakeholder_brief_missing_compliance.txt
evals.expected_output: A gap analysis that surfaces the compliance/regulatory gap the brief's title implies, alongside the other standard categories, each tagged with a severity tier reusing rfc-review's Blocking/Should-Fix/Nice-to-Have/Question scale.
assertions: A Compliance/regulatory finding is present given the fixture's missing-compliance framing
Each finding is tagged with one of Blocking/Should-Fix/Nice-to-Have/Question
No compliance concern is invented beyond what's inferable from the input's actual domain
